headline: Guy's 'I Don't Need A Manual' While Building IKEA Furniture, Now Has Extra Parts And A Leaning Shelf
summary: The shelf is now 'abstract art.' He calls it 'intentional asymmetry.' His wife calls it 'a hazard.'
fullBody: Jake McDIY refused to read the IKEA instructions. 'I have intuition,' Jake said. 6 hours later, he had extra screws, a missing shelf, and a bookshelf that leans 45 degrees. 'It's postmodern,' Jake claimed. His wife has hired a professional. The professional also couldn't fix it. The shelf is now 'sculpture.' Jake is no longer allowed to assemble furniture. He's now 'supervising' — which means 'watching and offering unhelpful suggestions.'
